content.summary: Plenful (plenful.com) is a San Francisco-based AI workflow automation platform founded in 2021 by Joy Liu, purpose-built for pharmacy teams and healthcare operations. The company automates high-value, complex workflows including 340B audit & optimization, prior authorization, order/referral intake, rebate management, and MFP (Manufacturer Fee-for-Service Program) intelligence. As of July 2026, Plenful partners with 95+ leading health systems, infusion practices, specialty pharmacies, and healthcare organizations nationwide, including MUSC, Cencora, Renown Health, Shields Healthcare Group, and Tampa General. The company raised a $50M Series B in April 2025 (co-led by Mitchell Rales/Danaher co-founder and Arena Holdings, with Bessemer Venture Partners, Notable Capital, TQ Ventures, Susa/Kivu Ventures, and Waterline Ventures), bringing total funding to $76M. LinkedIn shows approximately 120 employees (51–200 band) as of July 2026, with the company actively hiring across engineering, sales, and operations. The executive team includes Founder & CEO Joy Liu, CTO Gurkanwal 'GK' Brar, CPO Aman Shahi, CRO Michael Longo (ex-Veeva Systems, joined August 2025), CPeO Melissa Yeh (joined January 2026), SVP Pharmacy Tim L'Hommedieu, SVP Strategic Partnerships Dustin Eubanks, VP Sales Matt Savard, and VP Customer Success Liz Master. The website runs on Webflow; the ATS is Rippling; the core platform leverages agentic AI with Epic EHR integration. Key buying signals include fresh Series B capital deployed for product/GTM, 4x YoY revenue growth, rapid C-suite expansion, aggressive product launches (Referral Agent, GFI Agent in 2026), and market expansion into infusion and payor segments.
content.industry: Healthcare IT / AI Workflow Automation — Pharmacy & Healthcare Operations
tech_stack: Agentic AI / LLM-based workflow automation (proprietary platform)
Epic EHR integration (EpicCare Ambulatory — staff certifications documented)
AI-powered OCR / document processing (for messy, multi-format data ingestion)
content.headquarters: San Francisco, CA (with secondary office hub in New York City)
key_investors: Mitchell Rales (Co-Founder & Chairman, Danaher Corporation) — Board Member
buying_signals: Recent funding — $50M Series B closed April 30, 2025 (total $76M raised); capital earmarked for product development and go-to-market acceleration, signaling active vendor evaluation and spend.
Hyper-growth trajectory — 4x year-over-year revenue growth at time of Series B; customer base scaled from 60 (April 2025) to 95+ health systems by mid-2026.

Rapid C-suite build-out — CPO hired May 2025, CRO (formerly Veeva Systems) hired August 2025, Chief People Officer hired January 2026; leadership expansion typically precedes major tech procurement cycles.

Actively hiring across all functions — company careers page (ats.rippling.com/plenful/jobs) confirms active recruiting as of July 2026, suggesting demand for enabling tools (HR tech, productivity, collaboration).

New product launches — GFI Agent (July 2026), 340B Referral Agent (June 2026), ESP Submission automation (June 2026); rapid product roadmap execution indicates investment in engineering infrastructure.

Market expansion — moving beyond pharmacy/340B into infusion, specialty pharmacy, provider groups, and payor markets; attending NICA 2026 conference, signals new GTM motion and potential new buyer personas.
Technology migration opportunity — platform ingests messy, disparate data across formats and EHR systems (Epic and others); actively seeking integrations and workflow connectors.
Board-level engagement — Mitchell Rales (Danaher co-founder) joined board in January 2025; high-profile board signals accountability and professionalization drive.
Industry recognition — named to CB Insights Digital Health 50 (2024); covered by Forbes, TechCrunch, MobiHealthNews, Fierce Healthcare — elevated brand awareness driving inbound demand.
HQ office + NYC hub — dual-city presence as of 2026 suggests investment in physical workspace/IT infrastructure.
content.headcount_estimate: ~120 employees (LinkedIn, July 2026); LinkedIn company size band: 51–200 employees
